title: cell trace.deviations/trace.friction stay empty despite narrated deviations in outcome text/reports
detail: terminal-workspace-org-1 capped with trace.deviations:[] and trace.friction:null even though the cap outcome text and the worker report both explicitly describe 2 deviations (socket.rs, fake.rs). The structured fields meant to make deviations machine-discoverable for compounding/grooming passes weren't populated by the cap flow, so anyone querying trace.deviations programmatically misses real deviations that are only in prose.

title: bee-model-guard pins generation/extraction/review dispatches to read-only agent types that cannot execute cells
detail: Mid-session self-update introduced a hook requiring subagent_type bee-gather/bee-extract/bee-review for [bee-tier: ...] marker dispatches, but all 3 rendered .claude/agents/bee-*.md types are Read/Grep/Glob(+Bash for review)-only, no Edit/Write - they cannot implement+commit a cell as bee-swarming's own SKILL.md instructs. Blocks every execution-worker dispatch (full waves and AO14 single-worker tiny/small) until fixed upstream. Workaround in use: bare model param, no bee-tier marker, subagent_type general-purpose - works but is a workaround, not a fix.

title: concurrent-session commit race put an unrelated CSS fix (terminal-touch-scroll-1) inside agent-card-legibility-1's commit
detail: Mel (agent-card-legibility-1) and a concurrent session's terminal-touch-scroll-1 both had styles.css in their files list; Mel's git commit (81315d8) landed first and swept up the concurrent session's in-progress .term-viewport pointer-events hunk from the shared working tree, so dae9554 (terminal-touch-scroll-1's own commit) is empty of source changes even though its cell trace says files_changed:[web/src/styles.css] and verify_passed:true. No functional conflict (two independent additive CSS rules), but commit history now misattributes the change and trace.deviations on 81315d8 stayed [] despite the extra unplanned hunk - same class of bug as the earlier trace.deviations-not-populated friction filed for terminal-workspace-org-1. Root cause is likely that per-session file reservations aren't mutually exclusive across concurrently running bee CLI sessions in this checkout.

title: shared .bee/state.json phase/last_scribing_run field collides across concurrent sessions on different features
detail: agent-card-legibility's own scribing-run call was refused because a concurrent session's terminal-touch-scroll scribing-run had already advanced the single shared phase field to compounding (for its own feature) first. state.json has no per-feature scoping for phase/gates/last_scribing_run, so two sessions actively closing different features race on the same fields. Actual spec/backlog/decision work for agent-card-legibility completed fine via direct docs edits and bee.mjs decisions/backlog/reviews calls, which are not phase-gated the same way - only the scribing-run stamp itself (and its debt-clearing side effect) was blocked. Same root cause class as the earlier commit-race friction filed this session.
